title: Today's Markets Brief
summary: A quick look at the latest market news

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the latest market news. Let's dive in. I've got a few items on my radar today, and I'm excited to share them with you.

First up, Tempus AI is making waves with a record high short interest. According to a report, short interest in the stock has surged to an all-time high. For those who might not know, short interest refers to the number of shares that have been sold short but not yet covered. A high short interest can indicate that investors are betting against the stock, but it's also worth noting that it can lead to a short squeeze if the stock price starts to rise. This is always a fascinating story, as it shows how investors are positioning themselves.

Next, I've got my eye on the new Fed Chair, Kevin Warsh, who has big plans that could impact the market. With the stock market on fire lately, driven by AI euphoria, Warsh's plans could potentially put a damper on the Trump bull market. The S&P 500 has seen an 18% jump since its recent low on March 30, and the Dow Jones has followed suit. It's interesting to see how Warsh's plans will unfold and what they might mean for investors. I'm keeping an eye on this one, as it could have far-reaching implications.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
